dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8729 IH: No-Cost Breast Examinations in Medicaid Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Landsman (for himself and Mrs. Dingell) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce

form.official-title: To amend title XIX of the Social Security Act to require that diagnostic and supplemental breast examinations are furnished without cost sharing under the Medicaid program, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the No-Cost Breast Examinations in Medicaid Act of 2026.

section.header: Requiring that diagnostic and supplemental breast examinations are furnished without cost sharing under the Medicaid program

paragraph.text: Subsections (a)(2) and (b)(2) of section 1916 of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 1396o) are each amended—

subparagraph.text: in subparagraph (I), by striking or at the end;
in subparagraph (J), by striking ; and and inserting , or; and
by adding at the end the following new subparagraph:
diagnostic breast examinations and supplemental breast examinations (as such terms are defined in section 1905(ll)); and

paragraph.text: Section 1916A(b)(3)(B) of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 1396o–1(b)(3)(B)) is amended by adding at the end the following new clause:

clause.text: Diagnostic breast examinations and supplemental breast examinations (as such terms are defined in section 1905(ll)).

subsection.text: Section 1905 of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 1396d) is amended by adding at the end the following new subsection:

subsection.header: Definitions relating to breast cancer examinations

paragraph.text: The term diagnostic breast examination means a medically necessary and appropriate (in accordance with National Comprehensive Cancer Network Guidelines) examination of the breast (including, but not limited to such an examination using diagnostic mammography, breast magnetic resonance imaging, or breast ultrasound) that is—

subparagraph.text: used to evaluate an abnormality seen or suspected from a screening examination for breast cancer; or
used to evaluate an abnormality detected by another means of examination.

paragraph.text: The term supplemental breast examination means a medically necessary and appropriate (in accordance with National Comprehensive Cancer Network Guidelines) examination of the breast (including, but not limited to such an examination using breast magnetic resonance imaging or breast ultrasound) that is—

subparagraph.text: used to screen for breast cancer when there is no abnormality seen or suspected; and
furnished based on personal or family medical history or additional factors that may increase the individual’s risk of breast cancer.

subsection.header: Inclusion of screening mammography as a mandatory benefit

paragraph.text: Section 1902(a)(10)(A) of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 1396a(a)(10)(A)) is amended, in the matter preceding clause (i), by inserting (13)(D), after (13)(B),.
Section 1905(a)(13) of the Social Security Act (42 U.S.C. 1396d(a)(13)) is amended—

subparagraph.text: in subparagraph (B), by striking and at the end;
in subparagraph (C), by adding and at the end; and
by adding at the end the following new subparagraph:
screening mammography (as defined in section 1861(jj)) assigned a grade of A or B by the United States Preventive Services Task Force;

subsection.text: The amendments made by this section shall apply with respect to medical assistance furnished on or after the date that is 1 year after the date of the enactment of this Act.
